examples: Your BMR is defined as the amount of energy you burn while resting.
Your BMR is what your body needs to maintain normal functions like breathing and digestion.
One of the best benefits associated with exercise is that over time, you will increase your basal metabolic rate (BMR).
Mukesh Butani, chairman of consulting firm BMR Advisors, said the decision delivered an important critique of India's murky laws in areas such as taxes.
